focus: security second pass: deep-ai-council, deep-improvement, deep-research, and manual-testing-playbook surfaces

findingDetails.title: Model-benchmark workflow interpolates an unvalidated run label into shell commands
findingDetails.evidence: Both workflows place run_label in shell commands before any sanitizer; declared validation omits run_label and run-benchmark sanitizeLabel only protects a later history filename.
findingDetails.recommendation: Validate run_label with a strict basename allowlist before rendering and quote every derived path.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search covered both workflows and model-benchmark scripts; no pre-dispatch run-label sanitizer was found.

claimAdjudication.claim: The model-benchmark workflows can interpret a profile- or command-derived run label as shell syntax before the benchmark runner's filename sanitizer executes.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched both workflows and model-benchmark scripts for validation, slugging, sanitization, and shell quoting.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: The orchestration layer may derive run_label only from trusted profiles, but that invariant is not declared or enforced.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the renderer rejects shell metacharacters before command execution with an executable auto/confirm regression test.

results.summary: Guarded benchmark command claims omit a run-label shell boundary.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Cross-module workflows execute shell commands and mutation helpers across four mode packets.

selectedTargets: deep-ai-council scoped writer and replay helper
targetSelection.selectionReason: Security-sensitive command execution, filesystem mutation, environment override, and destructive sandbox boundaries in modules omitted from iteration 2.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A benchmark run label can reach shell syntax before path sanitization.

searchActions.queryOrPath: run_label|sanitizeLabel across model-benchmark workflows and scripts
searchActions.result: Both workflows interpolate run_label into shell commands; no workflow validation exists and runner sanitizer executes later for history filenames only.

searchLedger.rationale: Unvalidated data crosses a shell-command boundary in both execution modes.

searchLedger.invariant: An auxiliary payload outside ai-council must remain caller-owned and not bypass a production write boundary.

searchActions.result: The external payload is explicitly documented as caller-selected and no uncontrolled production workflow invocation was found.

searchLedger.rationale: The write is an explicit transport output rather than an implicit council artifact escape.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No untrusted production producer for the output path was identified.

searchLedger.hypothesis: An environment override permits untrusted arbitrary helper execution in production.

searchActions.result: Only the helper and its test fixture reference the override; no user-controlled production assignment was found.

searchLedger.rationale: Evidence supports a test hook in the inherited trusted process environment, not a newly exposed input boundary.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No production producer or untrusted environment assignment was found.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The sandbox setup cleanup can delete outside its intended temporary root.

searchActions.result: The script rejects relative, traversal, and non-/tmp values before cleanup; no followed-symlink escape was established.

searchLedger.rationale: The destructive target is lexically constrained and a terminal symlink is removed rather than traversed.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct read established guards before both destructive calls.

title: Model-benchmark workflow interpolates an unvalidated run label into shell commands

detail: Complex security review with strict graphless fallback coverage

reason: Explicit caller-owned transport output with no uncontrolled production producer found
Exact search found only helper and test-fixture use
Guard rejects relative, traversal, and non-/tmp targets before cleanup
